title: The House amid the Reeds · I
titleJa: 浅茅が宿　現代語訳・其の一

layers.label: Orientation
layers.description: The whole passage in English, before Japanese begins to enter.

layers.label: The place
layers.description: Three names establish the old landscape of Mamma.

layers.label: The man
layers.description: Katsushirō appears, and the opening sentence becomes Japanese.

layers.label: Inheritance
layers.description: The continuity of the household enters through three expressions.

layers.label: The fields
layers.description: The family’s material possessions begin to take Japanese form.

layers.label: Prosperity
layers.description: The inherited household reaches its former fullness.

layers.label: Temperament
layers.description: Three words reveal Katsushirō’s careless inward disposition.

layers.label: Farming
layers.description: The occupation and the burden he sees in it enter the page.

layers.label: The fall
layers.description: The complete modern-Japanese passage stands by itself.
